title: TASK-048 backend implementation
scope: Read canonical handoff and product direction documents
Keep backend work aligned to the canonical docs/PRODUCT_DIRECTION.md design
Prepare backend API and orchestration handoff evidence for mock provider publishing
Avoid claiming runtime server behavior while no executable backend module exists

backendImplementationQueue.item: Create runnable Spring Boot backend scaffold
backendImplementationQueue.details: Add build wrapper, application entrypoint, test setup, and package layout domain/application/infrastructure/presentation before endpoint work.
PUT/GET /api/onboarding/profile with validation for region, desired job, work type, gap months, resume flag, interview experience, interests, and default energy level.
POST/GET /api/quest-plans/today using a mock QuestAiClient that returns exactly three validated quests.
Complete, skip, fail, and redesign endpoints with explicit guards: completed quests cannot fail, redesign requires a stored failure reason, and child quests keep parentQuestId.
GET /api/dashboard/today and /api/dashboard/activity based on quest counts, next quest, and recent redesigns.

acceptanceCriteria: Related backend or orchestration files changed for the task purpose
Required verification commands run and reported
Remaining risks and QA points documented
qaChecklist: Parse github-artifact-manifest.json and agent-dispatch-package.json with ConvertFrom-Json.
Confirm the report does not claim Spring Boot runtime behavior or tests were completed.
Confirm the next backend task starts with a runnable scaffold before API implementation.
When code exists, verify the core flow: onboarding -> today quest generation -> fail reason -> redesign -> dashboard update.
reviewerChecklist: Check that the backend scope stays focused on the MVP redesign loop.
Block any implementation that parses LLM natural language instead of validating JSON DTOs.
Block wording that scores user willpower, risk, mental health, or sincerity.
Check controller/application/domain/infrastructure responsibilities once code is added.
